Title: Invoker — an agentic IDE

URL Source: https://invoker.build/about-us

Markdown Content:
[Invoker](https://invoker.build/)

## Linear + Superhuman

for orchestrating agents

Run agents in parallel with a kanban board, full IDE, and worktree support. Native, fast, keyboard first.

v0.0.1 · macOS 13+ · Apple Silicon required

[![Image 1: The Invoker task board](https://invoker.build/assets/stack-image-1-BiWgEDd5.png)](https://invoker.build/assets/stack-image-1-BiWgEDd5.png)

## A real workflow for multitasking agents

Start a task for every unit of work, see what's ready for review, and manage tasks from start → review → done.

### Ready notifications

Get notified when tasks are ready.

### Massively parallel

Run as many agents as you want.

### Handoff between tasks

Use @ to reference other tasks.

### Global search

Search every task you've run.

## Full built-in IDE

Every worktree gets a real IDE — editor, terminal, and a git diff viewer. All native, no app switching.

## Keyboard first

Vim for agents. Shortcuts for everything. Navigate tasks, send a reply, switch to editor and back. ⌘K command palette everywhere.

## Frequently asked questions

Which agents are supported?

Claude Code and Codex. Others are coming soon based on demand.

How do worktrees work?

You can create worktrees from the sidebar. Each worktree gets a task board and IDE with file tree, editor, and terminal. We don't support one worktree per task yet.

How is AI usage billed?

Invoker uses your existing `claude` / `codex` CLI. Your billing is handled directly by Anthropic or OpenAI using your plan with them. Your Invoker subscription is separate — we have monthly, annual, and lifetime [options](https://invoker.build/pricing).

Is there a free tier?

Yes. After the 3-day free trial, you get 1 hour of total agent time per day. A plan is needed for unlimited use.

## Ready to give it a try?

Invoker works with your existing Claude Code or Codex CLI.

[Download for Mac](https://download.invoker.build/Invoker.dmg)

v0.0.1 · macOS 13+ · Apple Silicon required
